expectations.text: Calls get_session_context() MCP tool to check for existing session state
expectations.evidence: Line 37: '**MCP call**: `mcp__ohno__get_session_context()`' — explicitly called during Phase 2 Session Initialization. Line 39 describes the return value: 'This returns previous session notes, current blockers, and any in-progress tasks.' Also listed in the MCP Tools summary table at line 529.

expectations.text: Calls get_next_task() to find the first available task
expectations.evidence: Line 68: '**MCP call**: `mcp__ohno__get_next_task()`' — called during Step 5 of session initialization. Line 70: 'ohno returns the highest-priority unblocked task.' Also called again at line 320 for task-002, showing correct loop behavior.

expectations.text: References semi-auto mode behavior (pause at story boundaries, skip task boundaries)
expectations.evidence: Line 306: 'Semi-auto behavior on task_complete: Log and continue (no pause).' Lines 369-412: Full story-complete PAUSE checkpoint with user options. Lines 495-503: Summary table explicitly showing Task complete = 'Log one-liner, continue automatically', Story complete = 'PAUSE -- wait for user input', Epic complete = 'PAUSE -- wait for user input'.

expectations.text: Mentions worktree setup or isolation for the task
expectations.evidence: Lines 120-156: Detailed worktree setup section including checking for existing story worktree, git worktree add command with story-based branch naming (story-001-user-registration), dependency installation, and announcement. Line 326: 'Same story (story-001) -- reuse existing worktree' for task-002. Lines 507-510: Worktree lifecycle summary covering story-scoped worktrees and in-place work for chore/docs.

expectations.text: Dispatches an implementer subagent for the first task
expectations.evidence: Lines 190-228: Full dispatch section titled 'Step 4: Dispatch Implementer Subagent' showing template variable assembly (TASK_ID, TASK_TITLE, etc.), Task tool dispatch with 'subagent_type: "pokayokay:yokay-implementer"', mode: 'bypassPermissions', and description of the implementer's TDD workflow (read codebase, write tests first, implement, self-review, commit).

expectations.text: Describes the checkpoint behavior for story completion
expectations.evidence: Lines 367-412: Full 'Checkpoint: Story Boundary (PAUSE)' section. States 'Semi-auto mode pauses at story boundaries.' Presents detailed checkpoint UI with story summary, worktree disposition options (merge/PR/keep/discard), next story preview, and user action choices (Continue/Review/Modify/Switch/End). Also lines 433-455 describe epic-complete checkpoint behavior.

claims.notes: File exists at plugins/pokayokay/agents/templates/implementer-prompt.md. Variable names plausible per CLAUDE.md architecture description.

claims.notes: Both agent files exist. Memory confirms: 'Split yokay-task-reviewer back into adversarial spec compliance + code quality.'

claims.notes: Agent file exists. Response correctly skips it for API-only task-001 (line 249) and triggers it for form component task-002 (line 347).

claims.notes: Both session-summary.sh and curate-memory.sh exist in hooks/actions/. CLAUDE.md confirms SessionEnd triggers sync and session-summary. Memory confirms curate-memory.sh enforces MEMORY.md budgets on SessionEnd.

claims.claim: Story tasks reuse the same worktree for related changes
claims.notes: CLAUDE.md states: 'Story tasks reuse the same worktree for related changes.' Response correctly shows task-002 and task-003 reusing the story-001 worktree.

claims.notes: Memory documents this as a bug fix (v0.15.2): 'added permissionMode: bypassPermissions to agent frontmatter' and 'mode: "bypassPermissions"' in dispatch instructions.

claims.notes: Line 17 claims 'No scope validation required (semi-auto does not require scope -- that's only enforced for auto/unattended/--continue)'. This is plausible per Memory ('--all: scope required for chaining') but would need skill/command source to fully verify.

eval_feedback: Excellent response. All 6 expectations are clearly met with explicit, detailed evidence. The response demonstrates deep understanding of the work-session skill by walking through the complete lifecycle: session init with get_session_context(), task acquisition with get_next_task(), worktree setup with story-scoped branching, implementer dispatch with template variables, semi-auto checkpoint behavior at task/story/epic boundaries, and post-session hooks. The response goes well beyond minimum expectations by covering skill routing, brainstorm gates, two-stage review, browser verification, auto-fix retry loops, worktree lifecycle management, and the full 9-task multi-story epic flow. All implicit claims verified against the actual codebase — referenced files (agents, templates, skills, hooks) all exist. The only minor note is that scope validation rules for semi-auto could not be fully verified without reading command source, but the claim is consistent with documented behavior.
